title: malva verticillata
intro: Malva verticillata, also known as the red mallow or marsh mallow, is a perennial plant species in the family Malvaceae. It is native to Europe, Western Asia, and North Africa, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and soothing effects. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems, skin irritations, and respiratory issues, due to its high mucilage content, which provides relief and protection to the mucous membranes. Malva verticillata can be prepared as an infusion, decoction, or salve, and is often used to calm digestive issues, soothe skin irritations, and ease respiratory problems.
